etymology.description: Depicts a pair of diverging lines -> stylized as part of 爲(為) (wéi, 'to do')
etymology.interpretation: The character 丷 is a simple pictogram that visually represents two separate lines or marks, which were stylized to form part of the more complex character 爲(為) (wéi, 'to do'). Though it appears in other contexts, its core depiction remains as intersecting or diverging lines.
